title: A Mix-Up at the Baby Clinic
summary: Laura and Beth are two moms who wanted to have babies together. They went to a special clinic in a place called northern Cyprus to get help. They picked one sperm donor so their kids would be like brothers and sisters. But when their son James was born, he looked different than expected. They got worried and did a DNA test. The test showed that both kids were not related to the sperm donor they chose. In fact, the kids were not even related to each other! This made the families very sad and confused. The BBC found other families with similar problems at clinics in northern Cyprus. These clinics are popular because they are cheap and have many donors, but they are not well controlled by laws. The families hope their story helps others be careful.

background_read: IVF is a way doctors help people have babies when it's hard to do naturally. It involves using eggs and sperm from donors. Sometimes, mistakes can happen at clinics, like using the wrong sperm. This can cause big problems for families who want their children to be related. In some places, like northern Cyprus, clinics might not follow strict rules, so it's important to check carefully before choosing one.
Article_Structure: WHO: Laura and Beth, two moms, and other families with kids from IVF clinics in northern Cyprus.
WHAT: A mix-up where the wrong sperm was used, so kids are not related to the chosen donor or each other.
WHERE: At IVF clinics in northern Cyprus, a territory not part of the European Union.
WHY: To show that mistakes at loosely regulated clinics can hurt families and warn others to be cautious.

perspectives.description: Families feel sad and worried because their children are not biologically related as planned, which affects their trust and happiness.
